title: centaurea saxicola
intro: Centaurea saxicola, also known as Mountain cornflower, is a perennial plant species in the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to provide several health benefits, including antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-cancer properties. Centaurea saxicola can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and support immune function.
